name: walking on stepmill
instructions: Adjust the stepmill machine to a comfortable level.
Step onto the machine and place your hands on the handrails for support.
Start walking by placing one foot on a step and then the other, alternating between legs.
Maintain an upright posture and engage your core muscles.
Continue walking for the desired duration or distance.
Gradually increase the intensity or speed as you become more comfortable with the exercise.
Remember to cool down and stretch after completing the exercise.

Adjust the stepmill machine to a comfortable level. Step onto the machine and place your hands on the handrails for support. Start walking by placing one foot on a step and then the other, alternating between legs. Maintain an upright posture and engage your core muscles. Continue walking for the desired duration or distance. Gradually increase the intensity or speed as you become more comfortable with the exercise. Remember to cool down and stretch after completing the exercise.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
